title: Make the fixed-width dashboard layout responsive
description:
# Issue #115 - Dashboard unusable on mobile

On phones the dashboard (starter/index.html + starter/styles.css) forces a 900px-wide layout: users must pinch-zoom and scroll sideways. There is no viewport meta tag and no breakpoint.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- index.html declares a viewport meta tag
- .container no longer hardcodes width: 900px (use max-width so desktop keeps its width)
- a media query at max-width: 768px stacks .layout vertically (flex-direction: column) and lets .sidebar span the full width
optimal_hint: Ideal fix: viewport meta in the HTML head; .container { max-width: 900px; width: 100%; }; @media (max-width: 768px) { .layout { flex-direction: column; } .sidebar { width: 100%; } }. The .content column should be flexible (flex: 1) rather than a hardcoded 650px.
